selected_axis.summary: Design v016 around a two-stage prompt interface: discover plausible candidates across the scene, then accept only detections with clear target-specific visual evidence and reject broad group boxes, row fragments, adjacent context, and unsupported extras.

why_this_axis: v015e preserved precision but failed dev recall against v014.
Dense scenes such as 66 and 84 show under-detection when the prompt is too conservative.
Case 67 shows that asking for more detections can become misplaced enumeration.
Cases 69, 86, 97, and 103 show local precision failures even when aggregate precision is acceptable.
Case 101 remains useful only as a manual diagnostic because broad-box behavior and reference-shape caveats complicate metric interpretation.
The offline structural guard simulator was too blunt and belongs to future scope.
future_prompt_authoring_constraints: Preserve categories, detection_guidance, bbox_format, and bbox_scale placeholders.
Do not paste raw human-report text into the runtime prompt.
Do not include case-specific examples in the runtime prompt.
Do not use scene-central-only wording that hides legitimate secondary visible targets.
Do not ask for blanket enumeration of repeated row-like fragments.
Reject broad group or scene boxes as final detections.
Require final detections to have target-specific visual evidence.
Preserve object-not-found abstention for protected controls.
Treat generated v016 output as candidate learning evidence until a later approved dev/holdout/promotion path.
success_criteria_for_later_authoring_wav: Hinge smoke improves or preserves v015e aggregate precision without repeating case 101 broad-box failure.
Dense recall pressure cases recover more valid targets than v015e without row-fragment enumeration.
Case 101 is manually reviewed regardless of aggregate metrics.
No holdout or all-112 run occurs without separate approval.
explicit_non_goals: No v016 prompt text in this package.
No v016 overlay in this package.
No runner session in this package.
